title: Unmet health care needs statistics

title: Health variables of EU-SILC

notes: Verified metadata page. Safe for definitions of self-reported unmet needs, medical care, dental care, reasons of barriers of access, percentage unit, persons aged 16+ in private households, and annual dissemination. Does not by itself verify every dataset table or regional completeness.

title: API - Getting started
relevance: Eurostat API overview for data and metadata access
verified_for: API retrieval; dataflow discovery; structure queries; base URI
notes: Verified documentation page. Safe for explaining that Eurostat supports Statistics, SDMX 3.0, SDMX 2.1, and Catalogue APIs, and that SDMX 3.0 can retrieve public datasets, structures, and data subsets.

title: API - Detailed guidelines
verified_for: API retrieval; detailed query documentation entry points
notes: Verified documentation page. Safe as a navigation source for Eurostat API detailed guidelines. Specific query syntax should be verified against the linked detailed subpages during data extraction.

title: European Health Interview Survey EHIS
relevance: Reference metadata for EHIS health determinants and unmet needs
verified_for: EU-SILC versus EHIS distinction; denominator and conceptual differences
notes: Verified metadata page. Safe for warning that EHIS and EU-SILC unmet-need indicators differ by question structure, denominator, age population, sequence, concept, and context.

title: Experts Group on Health Information - Update Eurostat Jan-May 2014
relevance: Official presentation mentioning revisions to EU-SILC health guidelines
notes: Verified PDF. It states there were changes of guidelines on unmet needs variables from 2015. It is not sufficient by itself to explain the resulting denominator or dataset publication consequences.

title: Time to reap the fruits of EU-SILC's new way of measuring unmet needs
relevance: Academic interpretation of the 2015 EU-SILC unmet-needs questionnaire change and later publication of new datasets
verified_for: Secondary evidence for methodology break and implications of hlth_silc_08b
notes: Use only as secondary evidence. It states the 2015 change split need from unmet need and that Eurostat datasets using the new method were published only at the end of 2023 with limited disaggregation.

title: Population on 1 January by age and sex
relevance: Population source used for target-population sensitivity
verified_for: Country-year total population; year-normalized population weights
notes: Extraction filter used in the reproducible script: freq=A, unit=NR, age=TOTAL, sex=T. Weights are normalized within year before regression or descriptive aggregation.
